overview.summary: Cloud-native SaaS platform built on a modular monolith architecture with clear domain boundaries, designed for progressive decomposition into microservices as the team and traffic grow.

context.businessContext: MVP must ship in 4 months with a team of 8 engineers. Architecture must support rapid iteration while maintaining the ability to scale to 10K+ concurrent users.
context.technicalContext: Greenfield project with no legacy constraints. Team has deep experience with TypeScript, React, and PostgreSQL.

constraints.impact: Limits feature scope and architecture complexity
All infrastructure must use AWS services
Security controls must be built in from the start

qualityAttributes.description: p95 API latency under 200ms, page loads under 1.5 seconds
Support 10K concurrent users at launch, 50K within 6 months
SOC 2 Type II compliance, E2E encryption, RBAC with SSO

frontend.rationale: React has the largest ecosystem and hiring pool. Zustand provides simpler state management than Redux. Tailwind enables rapid UI iteration.

backend.rationale: Fastify outperforms Express and has excellent TypeScript support. Prisma provides type-safe database access. Mercurius adds GraphQL for complex queries.

database.rationale: PostgreSQL for ACID transactions and rich query capabilities. Redis for sub-millisecond caching and WebSocket pub/sub fan-out.
database.schemaStrategy: Prisma migrations for schema versioning, module-scoped schemas

infrastructure.orchestration: ECS with CloudFront CDN and ALB
infrastructure.rationale: Fargate eliminates container host management. Aurora provides managed PostgreSQL with multi-AZ failover. Terraform enables infrastructure-as-code.

decisions.title: Modular Monolith over Microservices
decisions.context: Team of 8 needs to ship MVP in 4 months. Microservices add operational overhead disproportionate to team size.
decisions.decision: Start with a modular monolith using clear domain boundaries (modules: task, user, ai, notification). Each module owns its database schema and communicates via in-process events.
decisions.rationale: Reduces deployment complexity, enables rapid iteration, and provides a clear decomposition path when the team grows to 20+.

negative: Must enforce module boundaries via linting
Eventual decomposition requires event schema stability

decisions.title: PostgreSQL + Redis Dual-Store
decisions.context: Need ACID transactions for task state, plus sub-100ms reads for real-time features and pub/sub for WebSocket fan-out.
decisions.decision: PostgreSQL for persistent state with Prisma ORM. Redis for caching, session management, and WebSocket pub/sub.
decisions.rationale: Battle-tested stack with strong TypeScript tooling. Redis pub/sub enables horizontal WebSocket scaling without additional message broker.

negative: Redis persistence is secondary — data loss acceptable for cache layer

decisions.title: AI Engine as Isolated Module
decisions.context: AI prioritization is experimental and may change rapidly. Must not destabilize core task management.
decisions.decision: Encapsulate AI logic in a separate module with a stable interface (PrioritizationService). Module can call OpenAI API or local models interchangeably.
decisions.rationale: Isolates experimentation, allows A/B testing of algorithms, and enables fallback to rule-based prioritization.

negative: Must define stable priority scoring interface

decisions.title: WebSocket for Real-time with Polling Fallback
decisions.context: Need real-time task updates and presence indicators. Some corporate networks block WebSocket connections.
decisions.decision: WebSocket primary transport with automatic fallback to long-polling. Redis pub/sub for cross-instance message distribution.
decisions.rationale: Provides best UX for most users while maintaining compatibility with restrictive networks.

systemComponents.name: Web Client (SPA)
systemComponents.description: React single-page application providing the main user interface including task boards, focus mode, and dependency graph visualization

systemComponents.name: API Gateway
systemComponents.description: Fastify-based REST and GraphQL API serving the web client and CLI tool

systemComponents.name: Task Module
systemComponents.description: Core domain module handling task CRUD, sprint management, and dependency tracking

systemComponents.name: AI Prioritization Engine
systemComponents.description: Isolated module that scores and ranks tasks using ML models and rule-based fallbacks

systemComponents.name: Notification Service
systemComponents.description: Manages notification batching, focus mode filtering, and multi-channel delivery (in-app, email, Slack)

patterns.usage: Domain-driven module boundaries (task, user, ai, notification) with in-process event bus for cross-module communication
In-process event bus for decoupled module communication, enabling future decomposition into microservices
Read-heavy task listing uses optimized query paths separate from write-path mutations
Data access abstraction via Prisma repositories, enabling testability and future data store changes
AI algorithm selection between ML-based (OpenAI) and rule-based prioritization with feature flag control

projectStructure.description: Modular monolith organized by domain modules, each containing routes, services, repositories, and events
projectStructure.moduleOrganization: src/modules/{task,user,ai,notification}/ — each module has routes/, services/, repositories/, events/

structure.purpose: Shared utilities, types, and infrastructure code

dataFlow.description: Client -> API Gateway -> Module Router -> Service -> Repository -> PostgreSQL. Events published to in-process bus for cross-module communication. WebSocket updates pushed via Redis pub/sub.

security.overview: Defense-in-depth security architecture with JWT authentication, RBAC authorization, encryption at rest and in transit, and SOC 2 compliance path.

authentication.description: JWT tokens with refresh rotation. GitHub OAuth and email/password. SSO via SAML 2.0 for enterprise.
authentication.tokenStrategy: Short-lived access tokens (15min) + long-lived refresh tokens with rotation

authorization.description: Role-based access control with workspace-level roles (Owner, Admin, Member, Viewer). Resource-level permissions for sensitive tasks.

dataProtection.atRest: AES-256 encryption via AWS RDS and S3 server-side encryption
dataProtection.pii: PII masking in logs, GDPR data export and deletion support

compliance.implementation: In progress — audit controls being implemented
Data export and deletion endpoints, consent management

scalability.strategy: Horizontal scaling via ECS Fargate auto-scaling. Database read replicas for query-heavy workloads. Redis cluster for cache and pub/sub.

horizontalScaling.approach: ECS Fargate auto-scaling based on CPU and request count metrics

faultTolerance.strategy: Multi-AZ deployment with automated health checks
faultTolerance.failoverMechanism: Automated failover for database (Aurora), stateless services behind load balancer
faultTolerance.recoveryTime: < 5 minutes for database failover, < 30 seconds for service failover

errorHandling.strategy: Circuit breakers for external dependencies (OpenAI, GitHub, Slack)
errorHandling.retryPolicy: Exponential backoff with jitter for transient failures
errorHandling.circuitBreaker: Half-open after 30 seconds, trip after 5 consecutive failures

backupStrategy.frequency: Continuous Aurora backups with point-in-time recovery
backupStrategy.recoveryProcess: Automated point-in-time recovery, daily S3 snapshots for file storage

logging.strategy: Structured JSON logging to CloudWatch, aggregated in Datadog

metrics.strategy: Datadog APM for request tracing, custom metrics for task throughput and AI scoring latency

alerting.strategy: PagerDuty integration for P1/P2 incidents. Slack alerts for degraded performance. Runbook links in every alert.
alerting.escalation: P1: immediate page -> P2: 15-min Slack -> P3: daily digest

deployment.strategy: Blue-green deployment via ECS with automated rollback on health check failure

environments.name: production
environments.purpose: Live production environment with blue-green deployment

stages.name: Build
stages.purpose: Docker build and push to ECR

rollback.strategy: Automated rollback on health check failure
rollback.procedure: ECS reverts to previous task definition, ALB shifts traffic back to green deployment

integrations.name: GitHub
integrations.description: Link tasks to PRs, auto-update task status on merge, OAuth authentication

integrations.name: Slack
integrations.description: Task notifications, natural language task creation via slash commands, focus mode sync with DND

integrations.name: OpenAI
integrations.description: Task summarization, natural language parsing, priority signal extraction

findings.finding: Consider adding connection pooling for PostgreSQL to handle burst traffic
Redis single-instance is sufficient for launch but will need cluster mode at 50K users

implementationNotes: Start with Fastify single-process deployment, add clustering at 5K concurrent users
Use Prisma migrations for schema versioning — never modify production DB manually
AI module should have feature flag for gradual rollout
WebSocket reconnection must be transparent to the user — queue missed events and replay
